summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in LLMs, Robotics, and Research, with a focus on efficiency, security, and innovation. Google's Gemini models and NVIDIA's Vera Rubin architecture are leading the charge, while OpenAI and Anthropic are addressing security incidents and partnering with Hugging Face.
insight: The increasing use of AI-generated content is raising questions about authorship, ownership, and copyright, with companies like Sony and Udio facing lawsuits over AI-generated music, highlighting the need for clearer policies and regulations in the AI industry.
highlights: Google releases new Gemini models, including 3.6 Flash and 3.5 Flash-Lite, to improve efficiency and reduce token costs.
NVIDIA's Vera Rubin architecture is being used to build advanced AI factories, including Bristol Myers Squibb's SuperDuperPOD, to accelerate AI research and development.
OpenAI and Hugging Face partner to address security incident during model evaluation, highlighting the importance of collaboration and transparency in the AI industry.

articles.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its own pre-release models
articles.description: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

articles.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
articles.description: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

articles.title: Google’s Gemini 3.6 Flash targets enterprise agent token costs

articles.title: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
articles.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advance

articles.title: NVIDIA Vera Rubin Driving Performance Per Watt, Lowest Token Cost for Partners Worldwide

articles.title: OpenAI and Hugging Face partner to address security incident during model evaluation
articles.description: OpenAI and Hugging Face share early findings from a security incident during AI model evaluation, highlighting advanced cyber capabilities and lessons for defenders.

articles.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
articles.description: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

articles.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
articles.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

articles.title: China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself
